items.title: Genomics Is Coming To Malaysia, But Will It Reach Everyone? — Assoc Prof Dr Teguh Haryo Sasongko
items.summary: The real equity question is not whether genomic technology exists in Malaysia. It is whether a child in a public hospital, a patient in a rural clinic, or a family without savings can benefit from it when it matters. The post Genomics Is Coming To Malaysia, But Will It Reach Everyone? — Assoc Prof Dr Teguh Haryo Sasongko appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Two Industries Profit From The Same Bill: Malaysia’s Insurance-Hospital Cost Crisis — Dr Mohamed Rafick Khan
items.summary: If neither side of this (medical and insurance) industry disciplines its own pricing, the obvious remedy is one the PAC report gestures toward but doesn't go far enough in recommending: a mandated profit margin ceiling for both private hospitals and ITOs. The post Two Industries Profit From The Same Bill: Malaysia’s Insurance-Hospital Cost Crisis — Dr Mohamed Rafick Khan appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Nearly Nine In 10 Individuals Have Two Or More Cardio-Renal-Metabolic Risk Factors
items.summary: Policy briefs launched by National Cancer Society Malaysia and Boehringer Ingelheim highlights significant rates of co-existing cardiovascular, kidney, and metabolic diseases among Malaysians. The post Nearly Nine In 10 Individuals Have Two Or More Cardio-Renal-Metabolic Risk Factors appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Support Children With Cerebral Palsy With National Registry, Integrated National Rehabilitation Network — Dr Kelvin Yii
items.summary: Malaysia must establish a National Cerebral Palsy Registry and integrate a national rehabilitation network to foster collaboration between MOH, Socso rehab hospitals, and private rehab centres to deliver rehab services based on patient's clinical needs. The post Support Children With Cerebral Palsy With National Registry, Integrated National Rehabilitation Network — Dr Kelvin Yii appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Time To Implement School CPR, First Aid Programmes — Dr Sarah Shaikh Abdul Karim
items.summary: By weaving life-saving skills into our standard education system, we can support our dedicated educators and empower our students, ensuring our schools remain safe, nurturing environments. The post Time To Implement School CPR, First Aid Programmes — Dr Sarah Shaikh Abdul Karim appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Condemning Unfair Treatment Of Master’s Trained Specialists — Doctor
items.summary: The Master’s pathway should not become the pathway that demanded more, only to be told that it counts for less. MOH and JPA must stop punishing doctors for complying with their own training arrangements. The post Condemning Unfair Treatment Of Master’s Trained Specialists — Doctor appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Why Price Controls Over Private Hospitals Is A Bad Idea — Dr James Jeremiah
items.summary: Price control may protect inefficient hospitals. When the government fixes prices, weaker hospitals don't need to innovate; stronger hospitals can't differentiate properly; and patients lose the natural benefit of market discipline. The post Why Price Controls Over Private Hospitals Is A Bad Idea — Dr James Jeremiah appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Could Social Media Affect Who We Are?
items.summary: New research suggests that long-term Instagram use may influence the way people experience their own identity and distinguish themselves from others. The post Could Social Media Affect Who We Are? appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Pharmacists Say Credentialing, Legal Reform Needed Before Prescribing Expansion
items.summary: Pharmacists back micro-credentialing and a bigger role in patient care, as they already make many clinical decisions. But Malaysia needs clear training, credentialing and governance frameworks before expanding prescribing powers to pharmacists like the UK. The post Pharmacists Say Credentialing, Legal Reform Needed Before Prescribing Expansion appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Joint Ministerial Committee Touts Base MHIT Pilot By End July
items.summary: In response to the PAC's report, the Joint Ministerial Committee says the base MHIT product will be piloted by the end of July, claiming that "significant progress" has been made across its Reset strategy, including a phased adoption of DRG. The post Joint Ministerial Committee Touts Base MHIT Pilot By End July appeared first on CodeBlue .
